programmes.show_description: Paramedics treat a man with a fast heart rate and chest pains, a woman who has fallen over on her broken shoulder, and a man with an irregular heartbeat.
Alyce, who's 89, is brought to King's after falling near her home. Sean, who's 23, is rushed in after having a seizure that's lasted most of the night.
This episode shows how a trip to A&E can act as a long-overdue wake-up call. Phil, 36, has chronic pain in his back and side. Kamilla, 12, has been knocked down by a car.
On day two in Barcelona, Tara puts on some high-octane water sport fun and hits the city's top club at night. But will her idea of holiday heaven be anyone's holiday hell? (Ep12/20)
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
The group check in to the boutique Urban Beach B&B in Bournemouth, with its mod cons and deluxe bathrooms. And host Mark treats his guests to a speedboat ride along the Jurassic Coast.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien make an extraordinary find from a Dutch designer in accountant Debbie's lean-to.
Laura and Nick dream of making a permanent move to Estepona on Spain's Costa del Sol with their beloved dog Poppy. Can Jean Johansson find their perfect place for their £160,000 budget?
Windsor. Estate agent and horse racing fan Vikki hosts a ladies day-themed dinner party. But there's some cutting commentary and difficult diners. (Ep1/5)
Windsor. Radio DJ Mary serves up traditional Irish dishes, and an Eton mess with an Irish twist. But her evening becomes less about the craic, as cracks appear among the guests. (Ep2/5)
Windsor. Outspoken entrepreneur Mohinder hosts the third night. He's been dishing out criticism, so his guests are expecting big things... (Ep3/5)
Windsor. Retired sales manager and football fan Angie hosts a night of 1960s-inspired fun, featuring a rousing singalong, red cards galore and a curious card game. (Ep4/5)
Windsor. Car salesman Rob unleashes the mother of all surprises: his enormous country mansion, complete with swimming pool and Ferrari. And it completely upstages one guest. (Ep5/5)
At Gracelands Glamping in Ballyronan, couple Shaun and Hollie host with their newly built pods. But plumbing issues dampen the mood, and the barbecue breakfast gets a grilling!
At Manse on the Beach in Cloughey, former international rugby player Brian Robinson hosts. The guests love the sea views, but two of them feel hot under the collar after a bad slumber.
At The Secret Garden Glamping in Skelmersdale, Lancashire, friends Derry and Mike host. The B&Bers are ecstatic with their rooms, but two guests feel a little hemmed in by their hideaway.
The final visit is to the adults-only Hawthorn Hideaway in Walkeringham, run by Ronnie and Jo. Derry and Mike are delighted by the décor, while Shaun and Hollie get a stunning surprise.
On the final day, the B&Bers meet to discuss their feedback - from disappointing breakfasts, to pulling off the covers on some dodgy sleep scores - before the winner is announced.
VW Corrado: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend rescue Ashley's 1992 VW Corrado, which has been rotting away since he fell ill. Can the boys bring some joy back to Ashley's life?
Opel Manta GTE: In Rochdale, Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend rescue former amateur rally driver Alan's Opel Manta GTE. Can they re-ignite Alan's spark?
In the Peak District, Kirstie Allsopp helps Anne and her son Vinnie find a new home, while Phil Spencer searches for a dream property for Andrea and James.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Lauren and Christian's detached four-bed 'beige box' in beautiful Bury St Edmunds simply isn't working for them. Can Kirstie or Phil help?
The Murder of Rikki Neave: A true crime cold case special revisits the brutal, unsolved murder of a six-year-old in 1994. Will a compelling new lead finally reveal the killer? (Ep1/2)
The Murder of Rikki Neave: A dedicated police team start a fresh investigation into Neave's murder, sparking a cat-and-mouse game between detectives and a suspect on the run. (Ep2/2)
Paramedics treat a man who is under arrest but has hit his head, a six-year-old who's had a seizure, and a woman who may be having a stroke.
In this compilation episode focussing on older patients, paramedics treat a 103-year-old man who's collapsed and a driver who's had two crashes in a week but has no memory of what happened.
Some patients at King's have obvious symptoms while others initially baffle the medical team. Consultant Graham thinks Icylyn's bloated abdomen could be due to something serious.
Bobby, 48, is brought to King's by his partner after having seizures at home. Ronald, 70, is having problems breathing. Rienelle, who's seven, has fallen and landed on her back.
On day three in Barcelona, Angela takes her fellow guests to a vineyard to sample the local vino. The wine flows all day, but by dinner the conversation's a little worse for wear. (Ep13/20)
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
The B&Bers meet for one last time to find out how much they've been paid and to thrash out their differences. There's trouble as Mrs Tee and Alistair and Emma clash over their scores.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Surrey, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien meet petrolhead Graham, who collects Kawasaki motorbikes.
Gary and Jennifer are starting a new chapter in their lives by buying a holiday home near the Spanish town of Vera, on the Costa de Almería. Ben Hillman shows them what their £55k can buy.
Aberdeen. Glaswegian offshore logistics supervisor Michelle hopes to impress her guests with her 'Weegie' wit and the mother of all balloon games. (Ep1/5)
Aberdeen. Confident economics undergraduate David hosts with a 1920s Gatsby theme. His dress code goes down a storm, but will his student grub impress? (Ep2/5)
Aberdeen. Oil and gas buying specialist Sylvian hopes to wow her guests with her ambitious East-meets-West menu. But her toffee pudding turns out to be sticky in more ways than one. (Ep3/5)
Aberdeen. Comedy promoter Naz has his work cut out to pull off the winning party: he's managed to offend nearly all of his guests during the week, and he's not the greatest cook... (Ep4/5)
Aberdeen. Journalist Turid serves up a menu of home-grown produce, on a night featuring shocking revelations, farmyard antics and plenty of laughter. (Ep5/5)
The first visit of the week is to Abbey Court Guest House in Carlisle. Host Kelly aims to impress with plush rooms and abseiling. But will breakfast complaints send her chances off a cliff?
Glam couple Rob and Heloisa host at The Lime Tree Inn in Yorkshire, where chef Rob pulls out all the stops at breakfast. It's a shame the cleaners didn't do the same in Kelly's bathroom.
Visit three is to Holly Grove Farm in Staffordshire. Hosts Dave and Karon think that their rural glampsite is a tucked-away idyll, and their guests concur - apart from one...
Can host Pav handle his guests' grumbles about cleanliness and instant coffee when he hosts the week's final visit, at Beldon House B&B in Lincolnshire?
One B&Ber comes under fire on payment day for some suspicious scoring, while a confusing breakfast rating is high on the agenda for another. But most importantly, who's won the week?
Ford Escort RS2000: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend visit Dorset to fix up a Mk2 RS2000. It belongs to carpenter Mike, who gave up work to support his son who had a terrible accident.
Saab 900: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head to Loughborough to rescue Pravesh's 1992 Saab 900 Turbo that's in desperate need of repair.
Kirstie Allsopp helps Jay and Charlene upsize in Essex, and Phil Spencer's with Jemma and Graham, who returned from LA three years ago and now want to settle in Southend-on-Sea.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: In Croydon, Kirstie and Phil may have met their match: a swimming pool in the lounge. Can Kirstie help Christine love her bungalow or will Phil convince Tony to move?
Brand new series - Ghosts: A body is found at the home of Liguori's Uncle Edoardo, but the elderly eccentric blames the spirits that have been haunting him. In Italian/subs.
Kevin, who's 78, is rushed to A&E after going into cardiac arrest while exercising. Monica, who's 83 and has osteoporosis, has been rushed to King's after falling at home.
